# LLD — backlog

<!-- generated by /lld v2.27.0 on 2026-06-15 -->

**Feature:** `manual`
**Owner:** `ashwinimanoj@gmail.com`
**Status:** `draft`
**Linked PRD:** `n/a`
**Linked plans:** `[]`
**Version:** `0.1.0`
**Last updated:** `2026-06-15`

## §1 Overview {#overview}

`shield-backlog` is the Python package (`shield/backlog/shield_backlog/`) behind
the `/shield:backlog` skill. It captures, views, promotes, and reconciles ideas
held in the single global store at `docs/shield/backlog.json`.

Runtime shape: an importable library, not a daemon or HTTP service. Skills
`import shield_backlog` mid-task to capture ideas; `/backlog` command flows call
view/remove; `/plan` and `/implement` call the reconciliation triggers at
end-of-run. The package is `hatchling`-built (`shield/backlog/pyproject.toml`,
`shield-backlog` 0.1.0) and depends only on `jsonschema>=4.0`.

The public API (`shield_backlog/__init__.py`) exports `capture`, `read_backlog`,
`remove`, `eager_prune`, `lazy_sweep`, `kill_switch_enabled`, and
`BacklogInvalid`. Five modules back these: `store` (atomic read/write/remove),
`view` (text rendering), `suggester` (feature/epic candidates at capture time),
`reconciler` (the pure "epic landed" predicate), and `triggers` (eager/lazy
wiring plus the kill switch).

## §2 Scope & non-goals {#scope-and-non-goals}

**In scope:**

- The `backlog.json` store, schema, and validate-or-refuse read.
- `capture()` / `read_backlog()` / `remove()` with atomic write and
  compare-before-replace.
- Feature/epic suggestion at capture time (`suggester`).
- The pure reconciliation predicate (`reconciler.reconcile`) and its
  eager/lazy triggers (`triggers`).
- The `.shield.json` `backlog.auto_reconcile` kill switch and the
  `.shield/backlog-removed.log` recovery log.

**Out of scope:**

- The existing `backlog-store` LLD (`docs/lld/backlog-store.md`) — it documents
  the M1 store as a standalone `shield/scripts/backlog_store.py` module. This
  doc covers the consolidated `shield/backlog/` **package** (store + view +
  suggester + reconciler + triggers). The two are not duplicates: `backlog-store`
  is scoped to the store alone; this doc is the package.
- Priority buckets — `order` is a flat ascending integer; no priority tiers in v1.
- A live `migrate()` — read-old/write-new is documented only until schema_version 2.
- Multi-writer locking — single-actor assumption; correctness rests on atomic
  rename plus compare-before-replace, not a lockfile.
- The `/backlog` slash command and skill prose — owned by
  `shield/skills/general/backlog/SKILL.md`, not this package.

## §3 Module layout {#module-layout}

```
shield/backlog/
├── pyproject.toml                       unchanged  (shield-backlog 0.1.0; jsonschema dep; hatchling)
├── shield_backlog/
│   ├── __init__.py                      unchanged  (public API re-exports; LOCKED capture() signature)
│   ├── store.py                         unchanged  (capture/read_backlog/remove; atomic write; BacklogInvalid)
│   ├── view.py                          unchanged  (render(); pinned badge string)
│   ├── suggester.py                     unchanged  (normalize/suggest_feature/suggest_epic; Candidate)
│   ├── reconciler.py                    unchanged  (reconcile(); Verdict; RemovalDecision)
│   └── triggers.py                      unchanged  (eager_prune/lazy_sweep; kill_switch_enabled; load_*)
└── tests/                               unchanged  (test_store/view/suggester/reconciler/triggers + conftest)

shield/schema/
└── backlog.schema.json                  unchanged  (draft 2020-12; schema_version + entries[])

docs/shield/
└── backlog.json                         unchanged (runtime data)  the store itself

.shield/
└── backlog-removed.log                  unchanged (runtime data)  append-only recovery log
```

All files are pre-existing (`unchanged`); this LLD is a reverse-doc of the
shipped package. `SCHEMA_PATH`, `DEFAULT_BACKLOG_PATH`, `DEFAULT_REMOVED_LOG`,
`DEFAULT_MANIFEST`, and `DEFAULT_SHIELD_JSON` are all derived from
`Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]` (the repo root) in `store.py` and
`triggers.py`.

## §4 Data model {#data-model}

`backlog.json` is a single git-tracked JSON document, read in full per
operation. Source of truth: `shield/schema/backlog.schema.json` (draft 2020-12,
`additionalProperties: false` at both levels).

Top-level:

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `schema_version` | integer (≥1) | yes | `1` (`CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION`) | migration key; a value `> 1` is refused (`schema_version_too_new`) |
| `entries` | array of entry | yes | `[]` | ordered list; sorted by `order` then `id` on render |

Per entry (all required; `additionalProperties: false`):

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string (uuid4) | yes | generated via `uuid.uuid4()` | regex-pinned uuid4; cross-entry uniqueness enforced by the validator (`duplicate_entry_id`), not the schema |
| `order` | integer | yes | `max(existing.order, default=0) + 1` | ascending = view order; no priority buckets |
| `kind` | enum | yes | `task` (capture default) | `epic` \| `story` \| `task` |
| `source` | enum | yes | — (keyword-only, required) | `user` \| `agent` |
| `feature` | string (minLength 1) | yes | — | existing folder slug (`manifest.features[].name`) or proposed-new; the reconciliation key |
| `epic` | string (minLength 1) | yes | — | existing epic NAME (`plan.json.epics[].name`) or proposed-new; matched by name, never by positional `EPIC-N` id |
| `text` | string (minLength 1) | yes | — | the captured idea |

**Invariant — feature name == folder slug.** `manifest.features[].name` is
literally the feature directory name, so the reconciler derives
`docs/shield/<feature>/plan.json` without storing a path.

**Atomic-write store.** Every mutation rewrites the full document:

1. Read + validate the current store; snapshot `(schema_version, len(entries))`.
2. Apply the change in memory; re-validate.
3. Compare-before-replace: re-read the on-disk snapshot; refuse (`lost_update`)
   if it changed since step 1.
4. Write the full doc to a unique temp (`<name>.tmp.<pid>.<8-hex>`), `flush`,
   `os.fsync`.
5. `os.replace(tmp, path)` — atomic rename.

The JSON is serialized with `indent=2, ensure_ascii=False` plus a trailing
newline. No cache namespaces — there is no Redis or external store.

**Recovery log** (`.shield/backlog-removed.log`): append-only JSON-lines, one
record per recoverable removal: `{ts, rationale, entry}`. Written via the same
`flush`+`fsync` discipline *before* the destructive remove, so a replay restores
the entry.

**Migration policy.** `schema_version` is read on load. A newer version is
refused; v1 ships no `migrate()` — read-old/write-new becomes live at
schema_version 2.

## §5 API contracts {#api-contracts}

This is a library, not an HTTP service. "API" = the public Python functions
exported from `shield_backlog/__init__.py`. Each carries an `{#api-<slug>}` anchor.

#### capture {#api-capture}

```python
capture(text: str, *, kind: str = "task",
        feature: str | None = None, epic: str | None = None,
        source: str, path: Path | str | None = None) -> str
```

LOCKED signature (TRD §11, plan-review 2026-05-27). Appends one entry; assigns a
fresh uuid4 `id` and the next `order`; returns the id. `source` is keyword-only
and required so callers cannot positionally bind `kind` to `source`. `feature`
and `epic` must be non-empty (pass a proposed-new name, never `None`).

- **Request:** `text` (non-empty), `kind`, `feature`, `epic`, `source`,
  optional `path` override.
- **Response:** the new uuid4 id string.
- **Errors:** `BacklogInvalid` with code `missing_required_field` (blank
  text/feature/epic), `unknown_source_enum` (source ∉ {user, agent}), plus any
  read-side or write-side validation/`lost_update` failure.

#### read_backlog {#api-read-backlog}

```python
read_backlog(path: Path | str | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]
```

Loads and validates the store. A missing file returns an empty doc
(`{schema_version: 1, entries: []}`); a malformed store raises `BacklogInvalid`
(`invalid_json` / `schema_violation` / `duplicate_entry_id` /
`schema_version_too_new`). Never returns a partially-parsed doc.

- **Response:** the full validated document dict.
- **Errors:** `BacklogInvalid` (named codes above).

#### remove {#api-remove}

```python
remove(entry_id: str, *, path=None, log_to_recovery: bool = False,
       removed_log_path=None, rationale: str | None = None) -> dict | None
```

Mechanical remove-by-id. Returns the removed entry dict, or `None` if the id was
already absent (idempotent). When `log_to_recovery=True`, appends the entry to
the recovery log *before* the destructive remove. Reconciliation decisions live
in `reconciler` — this is the delete it calls.

- **Response:** removed entry dict, or `None`.
- **Errors:** `BacklogInvalid` (read-side malformed store, or `lost_update`).

#### eager_prune {#api-eager-prune}

```python
eager_prune(entry_id: str, *, backlog_path=None, manifest_path=None,
            shield_json_path=None, removed_log_path=None) -> RemovalDecision | None
```

End-of-run prune for one entry, driven by a promotion reference. Returns the
`RemovalDecision` when an action was attempted; `None` when the kill switch is
off or the entry was already absent (idempotent no-op — no log, no recovery
record). On a `REMOVE` verdict, calls `remove(..., log_to_recovery=True)`.

- **Response:** `RemovalDecision` or `None`.
- **Errors:** propagates `BacklogInvalid` from the underlying read/remove.

#### lazy_sweep {#api-lazy-sweep}

```python
lazy_sweep(*, backlog_path=None, manifest_path=None,
           shield_json_path=None, removed_log_path=None) -> list[RemovalDecision]
```

Walks all entries; removes each whose verdict is `REMOVE`. Exception-safe — a
single `BacklogInvalid` on one entry is caught and the sweep continues. Returns
the decisions that resulted in a removal (empty when the kill switch is off).

- **Response:** `list[RemovalDecision]`.
- **Errors:** none surfaced; per-entry `BacklogInvalid` is swallowed to preserve
  the sweep.

#### reconcile {#api-reconcile}

```python
reconcile(entry: dict, *, manifest: dict | None,
          plans: dict[str, dict] | None) -> RemovalDecision
```

Pure function — no I/O, no side effects. The single "epic landed" predicate
shared by both triggers. Returns a `RemovalDecision(verdict, feature, epic,
match_kind, gating_plan_json_path, reason)`. Verdicts: `REMOVE`,
`STAY_AMBIGUOUS`, `STAY_NO_MATCH`, `STAY_DOUBT`. Never raises on bad shape —
unrecognized manifest/plan input yields `STAY_DOUBT`.

#### suggest_feature / suggest_epic {#api-suggest}

```python
suggest_feature(text: str, *, manifest: dict | None) -> list[str]
suggest_epic(text: str, *, feature: str, plans: dict | None) -> list[Candidate]
```

Pure functions over already-read documents. Both match by normalized
(casefold + collapsed-whitespace) whole-word presence in `text`. Suggestion
never blocks capture — an empty list is valid. Ties (≥2 matches) are returned in
full; the caller auto-picks none.

#### kill_switch_enabled {#api-kill-switch}

```python
kill_switch_enabled(shield_json_path: Path | None = None) -> bool
```

Returns `False` only when `.shield.json` has `backlog.auto_reconcile = false`.
A missing or unparseable `.shield.json` defaults to `True` (auto-reconcile on).

## §6 Sequence flows {#sequence-flows}

#### Atomic capture {#flow-capture}

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant C as caller (skill or /backlog add)
  participant S as store.capture
  participant FS as filesystem
  C->>S: capture(text, kind, feature, epic, source)
  S->>S: validate args (text/source/feature/epic)
  S->>FS: read_backlog(path) — validate-or-refuse
  alt malformed store
    S-->>C: raise BacklogInvalid
  else ok
    S->>S: snapshot (schema_version, entry_count); append entry (uuid4, next order)
    S->>S: _validate_doc(doc) before any write
    S->>FS: re-read on-disk snapshot (compare-before-replace)
    alt snapshot changed
      S-->>C: raise BacklogInvalid (lost_update — no lost entry)
    else unchanged
      S->>FS: write unique .tmp + fsync; os.replace(.tmp, path)
      S-->>C: return new uuid4 id
    end
  end
```

#### Eager prune at end-of-run {#flow-eager-prune}

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant P as /plan or /implement
  participant T as triggers.eager_prune
  participant R as reconciler.reconcile
  participant S as store.remove
  P->>T: eager_prune(entry_id)
  T->>T: kill_switch_enabled()?
  alt switch off
    T-->>P: return None (no-op)
  else on
    T->>S: read_backlog; find entry by id
    alt absent
      T-->>P: return None (idempotent)
    else present
      T->>T: load_manifest + load_plans
      T->>R: reconcile(entry, manifest, plans)
      alt verdict != REMOVE
        T-->>P: return decision (entry stays; caller sees reason)
      else REMOVE
        T->>S: remove(id, log_to_recovery=True)
        S->>S: append recovery log BEFORE delete; atomic remove
        T-->>P: return decision
      end
    end
  end
```

#### Reconciliation predicate {#flow-reconcile}

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant Caller as eager/lazy trigger
  participant R as reconciler.reconcile
  Caller->>R: reconcile(entry, manifest, plans)
  R->>R: manifest shape ok? entry has feature+epic?
  alt bad shape / missing fields
    R-->>Caller: STAY_DOUBT (never remove on doubt)
  else ok
    R->>R: feature in manifest with plan_json?
    alt absent or prd-only
      R-->>Caller: STAY_NO_MATCH
    else has plan.json
      R->>R: normalize(epic); find features whose plan has matching epic name
      alt 0 matches
        R-->>Caller: STAY_NO_MATCH
      else >=2 matches (cross-feature collision)
        R-->>Caller: STAY_AMBIGUOUS
      else exactly 1
        alt match in a different feature
          R-->>Caller: STAY_NO_MATCH
        else match is the entry's feature
          R-->>Caller: REMOVE (+ gating_plan_json_path)
        end
      end
    end
  end
```

## §7 Error handling {#error-handling}

All refusals surface as `BacklogInvalid`, carrying a stable `.code` and
`.message`. No HTTP status applies (library). `reconcile()` never raises — it
encodes doubt as a verdict.

| Code | Raised when | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `invalid_json` | store is not parseable JSON (read, or on-disk during compare-before-replace) | refuse; surface to caller; never read partial |
| `schema_violation` | JSON Schema rejects the doc (default mapping) | refuse on read and on write-side validate |
| `missing_required_field` | required field absent, or capture given blank text/feature/epic | refuse before writing any `.tmp` |
| `unknown_kind_enum` | `kind` ∉ {epic, story, task} | refuse (schema-mapped) |
| `unknown_source_enum` | `source` ∉ {user, agent} (capture arg or on-disk) | refuse |
| `invalid_id_format` | `id` not a uuid4 (read-side) | refuse |
| `duplicate_entry_id` | two entries share an `id` (validator check, not schema) | refuse |
| `schema_version_too_new` | on-disk `schema_version > 1` | refuse; never downgrade |
| `lost_update` | compare-before-replace finds a changed on-disk snapshot | refuse the replace; no lost entry |
| write IO error mid-`.tmp` | disk/permission failure during temp write | unique `.tmp` left orphaned, never `os.replace`d; canonical store never partial |

`remove` on an absent id is **not** an error — it returns `None` (idempotent).
`lazy_sweep` catches per-entry `BacklogInvalid` and continues.

## §8 Concurrency & state {#concurrency-and-state}

Single-writer assumption (TRD §6 N1/N5) — no lockfile. Correctness rests on
three mechanisms in `store.py`:

1. **Atomic rename:** full doc → unique `.tmp` (`<name>.tmp.<pid>.<8-hex>`) →
   `fsync` → `os.replace()`. A reader never sees a half-written file.
2. **Validate-or-refuse** on every read and before every write — a malformed or
   partial store is rejected, never silently truncated.
3. **Compare-before-replace:** the `(schema_version, entry_count)` snapshot
   captured at read time is re-checked just before `os.replace()`. A capture
   racing a reconciliation remove — the real lost-update threat if the
   single-writer assumption is violated — fails loudly with `lost_update`
   instead of dropping an entry.

**Named race:** capture-vs-remove on the same store. Resolution: compare-before-
replace turns the loser into a loud refusal. The recovery log is written *before*
the destructive remove, so even a crash between log-append and rename leaves the
entry recoverable.

**State transitions:** an entry is born on `capture`, viewed via `render`, and
leaves on `remove` (manual, eager, or lazy). The reconciler is a pure verdict
function over external state (`manifest.json`, per-feature `plan.json`) and owns
none itself. Externalized state: the store file, the recovery log, and the
`.shield.json` kill switch.

## §9 Configuration {#configuration}

<details open>
<summary>§9 Configuration</summary>

The component reads one user-tunable setting plus path overrides. Lifted because
the kill switch is an operator-facing control (PRD risk mitigation).

| Name | Type | Default | Range | Secret | Hot-reload |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `.shield.json` → `backlog.auto_reconcile` | boolean | `true` | `true` \| `false` | no | yes — read fresh per trigger call (`kill_switch_enabled`) |
| `SHIELD_BACKLOG_PATH` (env) | path | `docs/shield/backlog.json` | any path | no | yes (read-only view/validate flows per SKILL.md) |
| `path` / `backlog_path` / `manifest_path` / `shield_json_path` / `removed_log_path` (call args) | path | repo-root-derived defaults | any path | no | n/a (per-call) |

Setting `auto_reconcile=false` disables both eager prune and lazy sweep; manual
`remove()` still works. A missing/unparseable `.shield.json` defaults to
enabled.

</details>

## §10 Observability {#observability}

- **Logs:** no logging framework is wired in v1 (no telemetry — PRD §7). The
  observable trail is the **recovery log** (`.shield/backlog-removed.log`):
  one JSON-line per recoverable removal with `{ts (UTC ISO, seconds),
  rationale, entry}`. `rationale` is prefixed by the trigger
  (`eager_prune: ...` / `lazy_sweep: ...`).
- **Metrics:** none in v1. Entry count is derivable from the store
  (`len(entries)`); removal count is derivable from the recovery log line count.
- **Traces:** n/a — single-process library calls; no distributed spans.

The `RemovalDecision` returned to callers carries the F9 fields (`feature`,
`epic`, `match_kind`, `gating_plan_json_path`, `reason`) so the calling
command can render an identical structured line across eager and lazy paths.

## §11 Security & privacy {#security-and-privacy}

<details>
<summary>§11 Security & privacy</summary>

n/a — the store and recovery log hold developer-authored idea text only (no PII,
no auth surface). Both are plaintext, git-tracked (store) / local (`.shield/`
log) files inside the repo, sharing the same trust boundary as the rest of
`docs/shield/`. No authentication, authorization, or data-classification
surface beyond the filesystem permissions of the checkout.

</details>

## §12 Performance & scaling {#performance-and-scaling}

#### §12.1 Load {#load}
A few capture/remove ops per Shield session; one `view` per `/backlog` invocation;
one eager prune per promoted entry at `/plan`-`/implement` close; one lazy sweep
per `/backlog view`. Backlog expected ≤ ~200 entries (audit cadence in SKILL.md
triggers scope review beyond that). Payloads are tens of KB.

#### §12.2 SLO {#slo}
Capture, read, and view complete in ≪ 100ms for a ≤200-entry store; contributes
to the TRD §6 N2 ~1s view budget. Availability is local-filesystem only — no
network dependency, no uptime target.

#### §12.3 Bottleneck {#bottleneck}
IO-bound: one full-file read plus one `os.replace()` per mutating op; `fsync`
dominates write latency. Never CPU-bound at this scale — JSON parse/validate is
microseconds for ≤200 entries.

#### §12.4 Latency breakdown {#latency-breakdown}
Per capture/remove: one read + parse + validate, one compare-before-replace
re-read, one temp write + `fsync`, one `os.replace()`. `fsync` is the largest
contributor. `n/a — finer numbers measured post-ship; targets in §12.2 SLO`.

#### §12.5 Capacity {#capacity}
A ≤200-entry JSON doc is tens of KB — trivial memory; whole doc held in memory
per op. No connection pool, no threads.

#### §12.6 Scale-out lever {#scale-out-lever}
n/a — single local file, single actor; no horizontal scaling dimension. The
single-writer assumption (no lock) is the deliberate constraint; revisit with a
lockfile only if Shield becomes multi-actor.

#### §12.7 Caches {#caches}
One in-process schema cache (`_SCHEMA_CACHE` in `store.py`) avoids re-reading
`backlog.schema.json` per call; it is process-lifetime and never invalidated
(the schema is static). The store itself is **not** cached — every op reads the
file fresh (validate-or-refuse), so there is no store-cache invalidation problem.

#### §12.8 Degradation {#degradation}
On a malformed store, `read_backlog`/`capture`/`remove` refuse with
`BacklogInvalid` rather than degrade — the user fixes or reverts the file. On
doubt about reconciliation inputs, `reconcile` returns `STAY_DOUBT` and the
entry stays (never a wrong removal). `lazy_sweep` skips a bad entry and continues
the rest of the sweep. No partial-write state is ever observable.
